key_insight: The paper introduces a fully open, multilingual pipeline that converts large news archives into signed, temporal political-elite knowledge graphs by jointly extracting entities and relations across languages.
engineering_relevance: It enables scalable, reproducible information extraction for building structured datasets from unstructured text, reducing manual coding effort and supporting cross-national computational social science.
method: Span-based multilingual NER identifies person and organization mentions.
A three-stage linking cascade resolves mentions to Wikidata IDs across languages.
An ontology-constrained mixture-of-experts model with guided decoding extracts directed, signed relations.
evidence_signal: Against a 3,491-relation gold standard, the pipeline achieved 68.2% strict and 93.7% lenient textual correctness, and case studies in Austria and Poland matched public-record events.
watch_item: Performance depends on robust entity resolution and ontology coverage, so linking errors or domain-specific relation types can propagate through the downstream knowledge graph.
paper_title: Mapping Political-Elite Networks in Europe with a Multilingual Joint Entity-Relation Extraction Pipeline
